design: Train de-novo antigen axis + logistic on Gide (n=30), freeze train-fit standardization + weights, apply to held-out cohort.

gide_to_hugo_heldout._note: Full Hugo cohort (28/28 runs). Was n=12 pilot (ant6=0.583, CI[0.22,0.92]); completing the cohort tightened CI to [0.37,0.80] with point estimate ~unchanged.

conclusion: The Gide-trained de-novo antigen axis fails to transfer to BOTH independent held-out cohorts: AUROC 0.36-0.44 on Riaz (n=10) and ~0.59-0.60 on Hugo (n=28, 95% CI [0.37,0.80] still includes chance), vs within-Gide 0.87. Completing the Hugo cohort from the n=12 pilot to the full 28 runs LEFT THE PILOT REGIME: the held-out AUROC point estimate is essentially unchanged (0.583->0.595) while the 95% CI tightened from [0.22,0.92] to [0.37,0.80]. This converts the Hugo arm from 'underpowered, undecidable' to a POWERED non-replication - the axis genuinely does not transfer to Hugo, it is not merely underpowered. This strengthens the core conclusion: a gene-level de-novo antigen-presentation axis is cohort-specific and not a transferable tumor-intrinsic RNA state.

_refresh_provenance.method: Identical to original generator (frame f16431e3, cells 245-248): train-fit z-standardized mean-panel axis on Gide n=30, logistic map, applied to held-out Hugo; bootstrap CI 2000 resamples seed=1. n=12 control reproduced exactly before refreshing.
